title: AGENTS block baseline gate + onboarding capture path
action: Per D2: AGENTS.block.md Startup gains step 6 — 'If .bee/config.json records commands.verify, run it once per session before claiming any cell; a red baseline is surfaced to the user and becomes its own fix-first tiny cell — never build on red.' Session finish gains the mirror item: 'If commands.verify is recorded, run it; a red result is reported, never silently left.' Per D4: onboarding gains a skippable capture step — when .bee/config.json lacks commands, onboard output (and --json result) includes a prompt-worthy notice instructing the agent to ask the user for setup/start/test/verify and write them to config.json; never invents commands, never blocks completion. Follow onboard_bee.mjs's existing managed-version mechanism for the AGENTS block content change (bump whatever version marker the installer uses so re-onboarding refreshes the block). Extend test_onboard_bee.mjs: block contains the baseline-gate step; onboarding on a repo without commands surfaces the capture notice; idempotency suite stays green.

truths: Re-onboarding an existing repo refreshes the AGENTS block to include the baseline-gate step without touching content outside BEE markers
Onboarding completes successfully with or without commands recorded

artifacts.substantive: startup step 6 + session-finish mirror item present, conditional on commands.verify
assertions for block content and capture notice

key_links: onboard_bee.mjs version marker bumped so managed-file refresh fires
prohibitions: Never write AGENTS.md content outside the BEE:START/END markers

trace.outcome: AGENTS block startup step 6 (baseline gate, conditional on commands.verify) + session-finish mirror; onboarding emits skippable capture notice in plan and apply payloads when commands absent; suite green, block refresh via content-hash managed versions (no manual bump needed).
